name: Buganda
title: Kabaka
notes: Largest and most influential kingdom. Abolished by Milton Obote in 1967 (republican constitution). Restored in 1993 by Yoweri Museveni as a cultural institution without political power. The Lukiiko (parliament) continues to function.

name: Bunyoro-Kitara
title: Omukama
notes: One of the oldest kingdoms in East Africa, traces origins to the Bachwezi dynasty. Once the dominant power in the Great Lakes region. Lost 'lost counties' to Buganda in colonial era, partially restored 1964. Abolished 1967, restored 1993.

name: Toro
current_ruler: Omukama Oyo Nyimba Kabamba Iguru Rukidi IV
title: Omukama
notes: Founded c. 1830 when Prince Kaboyo broke away from Bunyoro. Abolished 1967, restored 1993. King Oyo was crowned at age 3 in 1995, making him one of the world's youngest monarchs at the time.

name: Ankole
title: Omugabe
notes: Unlike Buganda, Bunyoro, and Toro, the Ankole kingdom has NOT been officially restored due to internal disagreements between Bairu (farmers) and Bahima (pastoralists). Abolished 1967. Restoration remains politically sensitive.

name: Busoga
current_ruler: Kyabazinga William Wilberforce Gabula Nadiope IV
title: Kyabazinga
notes: A confederation of chieftainships rather than a centralized kingdom. The Kyabazinga title is a rotating one among clan chiefs. Abolished 1967, restored 1995. Located in eastern Uganda at the source of the Nile.
